title: Listen and Choose a Response — Final Set

questions.stem: Do I need to take Statistics 101 before enrolling in Econometrics?

options.A: Yes, Statistics 101 is a strict prerequisite for Econometrics.
options.B: Econometrics is offered every fall semester.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about course prerequisites, so a clear confirmation or denial with details is the most helpful response.

questions.stem: I'm sorry I missed our study session. I completely lost track of time.

options.A: No worries. Let's reschedule for tomorrow afternoon.
options.B: You should set reminders on your phone.

questions.explanation: The speaker is apologizing for missing a meeting, so graciously accepting the apology and proposing a new time is constructive.

questions.stem: Would you be willing to write me a letter of recommendation for grad school?

options.A: I'd be happy to. Please send me your resume and statement of purpose.
options.B: Grad school applications require several letters.

questions.explanation: The speaker is requesting a letter of recommendation, so a positive response with instructions on needed materials is appropriate.

questions.stem: I'm not sure which major to choose. Everything seems interesting right now.

options.A: That's completely normal. Have you talked to the career counseling center?
options.B: You should decide before your sophomore year.
options.C: Some majors are more practical than others.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing uncertainty about a major decision, so normalizing their feelings and suggesting a helpful resource is supportive.

questions.stem: Did the seminar time change? I thought it was at 10 AM, but my calendar says 1 PM.

options.A: Yes, it was moved to 1 PM because the speaker's flight was delayed.
options.B: Seminars are usually scheduled in the morning.
options.C: You should check the department website.

questions.explanation: The speaker is confused about a schedule discrepancy, so confirming the change and providing the reason is helpful.

questions.stem: We're going hiking this weekend. Do you want to join?

options.A: I'd love to, but I have a family commitment this weekend. Maybe next time?
options.B: Hiking is too exhausting for me.

questions.explanation: The speaker is extending a social invitation, so politely declining with a reason and leaving the door open is courteous.

questions.stem: Is there an extra fee for using the 3D printing lab?

options.A: Yes, it's five dollars per hour of printing time.
options.B: The 3D printing lab has new equipment.
options.C: Labs are open to all engineering students.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about fees, so providing the specific cost structure directly answers their question.

questions.stem: I was so worried about the exam, but I actually think I did okay!

options.A: I'm glad to hear that! All that studying paid off.
options.C: The exam was easier than last year's.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing relief after a stressful event, so sharing in their relief and validating their effort is encouraging.

I'm sorry I missed our study session. I completely lost track of time.
Would you be willing to write me a letter of recommendation for grad school?
I'm not sure which major to choose. Everything seems interesting right now.

Is there an extra fee for using the 3D printing lab?
